answer:
\boxed{F}

A double-bind message conveys contradictory or conflicting expectations, leaving the recipient unable to respond without making someone unhappy. Option **F** exemplifies this because the father’s verbal encouragement ("I sure hope you can come") clashes with his nonverbal cues (tone/posture), creating an impossible situation. The other options either lack the conflicting verbal/nonverbal elements or are more straightforward warnings rather than true double-binds.
